title: Women Talking
logline: After discovering they've been systematically drugged and assaulted, the women of an isolated religious colony must decide whether to stay and fight, do nothing, or leave everything behind.
protagonist: The women collectively (Ona, Salome, Mariche, Greta, Agata)
antagonistOrConflict: The patriarchal system and the men who have assaulted them

actBreaks.description: The women settle into their debate in the hayloft after establishing the three options and their stakes.

validation.ruling: Scene 22 establishes the fundamental conflict between staying to fight versus leaving, with Greta and Salome's opposing viewpoints creating an irreversible shift that forces the story into higher stakes debate and planning.

actBreaks.description: Ona articulates the philosophical heart of their decision about what kind of world they want to create.

validation.ruling: Scene 67 shows Scarface Janz turning away from Neitje's request, creating doubt about whether the 'do nothing' women will inform on them, fundamentally changing the stakes as the women must now act without certainty of safety.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This is a chamber piece masquerading as an epic. The structure is deceptively simple—women talking in a hayloft—but it operates like a pressure cooker, with each scene deepening the philosophical and emotional stakes. The script uses a circular, almost liturgical rhythm, returning repeatedly to the central hayloft location while weaving in flashbacks that reveal the trauma's scope. The pacing is deliberately contemplative, allowing ideas to breathe and develop organically through dialogue rather than action.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The script maintains tension through philosophical debate rather than physical action. Scenes 22-67 form the heart of the deliberation, with flashbacks (scenes 23, 25, 27, 52, 54) providing emotional punctuation. The final act accelerates as the decision crystallizes, culminating in the departure sequence (scenes 99-102).

incitingIncident.description: Over a hundred women take turns voting at a crudely built ballot box, marking X's next to Neitje's drawings while coffee and food are served.

validation.ruling: Scene 11 shows the women of the colony voting on their response to the attacks, establishing the central conflict and forcing them into action that cannot be ignored - this vote shatters their ordinary world and sets the main story in motion.

midpoint.description: Mejal stands up from bed one year earlier, blood dripping to the floor, then begins screaming as Agata prays over her voice-over.
midpoint.sceneHeading: 52 INT. MEJAL’S ROOM - MORNING - ONE YEAR EARLIER 52 52

validation.ruling: Scene 54 is a flashback that doesn't drive present action forward. Scene 53 better serves as midpoint where the women shift from reactive discussion to proactive planning, with Salome's passionate defense of protecting their children marking a point of no return in their commitment to leave.

climax.description: Nettie/Melvin and children in the field listen to the distant singing, with some children joining in while others continue playing obliviously.

validation.ruling: Scene 95 shows violence against Scarface Janz but isn't the ultimate confrontation. Scene 85 is the final hayloft meeting where the women make their ultimate decision and August says goodbye to Ona, representing the peak emotional confrontation and resolution of the main story question.

scenes.heading: 1 EXT. SCHOOLHOUSE - MORNING 1 1
scenes.summary: Aaron balances on a fence while 14 boys and teacher August watch silently, wondering if he will fall.

scenes.heading: 2 EXT. ROAD - MORNING 2 2
scenes.summary: Seven women walk along a dusty road as wind picks up and they hold onto their hats.

scenes.heading: 3 INT. CHURCH - MORNING 3 3
scenes.summary: Dozens of men bow in prayer while August's voiceover explains that women woke up bruised and bleeding, with many believing they were being punished for sins.

scenes.heading: 4 EXT. FIELD - SUNSET (ONE WEEK PRIOR TO THE VOTE) 4 4
scenes.summary: Ona, Salome, Miep, and August sit in a field discussing how the attacks happened hundreds of times and weren't dreams.

scenes.heading: 5 INT. SALOME'S KITCHEN - NIGHT 5 5
scenes.summary: Neitje draws three pictures representing their options while the women watch: 'Do nothing,' 'Stay and fight,' and 'Leave.'

scenes.heading: 6 EXT DILAPIDATED BARN - MORNING 6 6
scenes.summary: August walks in circles holding a gun until Ona approaches and he tries to hide it, then she tells him about seeing a squirrel.

scenes.heading: 7 EXT. FIELD - MORNING 7 7
scenes.summary: Nettie/Melvin, dressed as a man, plays tag with 13 children including Julius in a field.

scenes.heading: 8 INT. HAYLOFT - EARLY AFTERNOON 8 8
scenes.summary: The women remove their shoes and socks in the hayloft, revealing rope burns on some ankles, while Agata's voiceover references washing disciples' feet.

scenes.heading: 9 EXT. COLONY ROAD - DAWN 9 9
scenes.summary: Colony men including Peters and Klaas load buggies in a convoy while women help but avoid eye contact, with Peters announcing they'll return in two days.

scenes.heading: 10 INT. HAYLOFT - EARLY AFTERNOON 10 10
scenes.summary: August introduces himself in voiceover as the schoolteacher taking minutes because the women are illiterate, explaining he agreed because he loves Ona and had nothing to do but kill himself.

scenes.heading: 11 INT. BARN - MORNING 11 11
scenes.summary: Over a hundred women take turns voting at a crudely built ballot box, marking X's next to Neitje's drawings while coffee and food are served.
scenes.turningPointNote: The formal vote that sets the central conflict in motion - tied between staying/fighting and leaving

scenes.heading: 12 OMITTED 12 12
scenes.summary: Scene omitted.

scenes.heading: 13 OMITTED 13 13
scenes.summary: Scene omitted.

scenes.heading: 14 INT. HAYLOFT - EARLY AFTERNOON 14 14
scenes.summary: Representatives from the Reimers and Friesens families gather in the hayloft with the Janz family to decide between staying/fighting or leaving after the vote tied, while Scarface begins to speak.

scenes.heading: 15 INT. GRETA’S HORSE BARN - EARLY MORNING 15 15
scenes.summary: Greta lovingly tends to her horses Ruth and Cheryl, brushing them and looking into their eyes before walking away with sadness, saying 'We'll go.'

scenes.heading: 16 EXT. ROAD - EARLY MORNING 16 16
scenes.summary: Greta drives her buggy when a Rottweiler appears and barks, causing Ruth and Cheryl to bolt as she struggles to control them.

scenes.heading: 17 INT. HAYLOFT - EARLY AFTERNOON 17 17
scenes.summary: The women debate whether they are animals, with Greta arguing they've been preyed upon like animals and should respond accordingly, while Salome asks if she means run away or kill attackers.

scenes.heading: 18 EXT. SHED - 2 DAYS EARLIER - AFTERNOON 18 18
scenes.summary: Salome runs shrieking with a scythe at a shed containing five men, breaks the lock, slashes at one man before being pulled away by Peters who then decides to go to the city.

scenes.heading: 19 INT. HAYLOFT - EARLY AFTERNOON 19 19
scenes.summary: The women debate forgiveness and whether they'll be allowed into heaven, with Salome laughing at Scarface's concerns about forgiveness while Mariche worries about being forced to leave the colony.

scenes.heading: 20 EXT. BARN - AFTERNOON 20 20
scenes.summary: The sun is slightly lower in the sky outside the barn.

scenes.heading: 21 INT. SCARFACE JANZ’S HOUSE - AFTERNOON 21 21
scenes.summary: Scarface Janz sits at her kitchen table looking haunted while her family does chores, then walks outside to stare at the hayloft and horizon as Anna shakes with panic.

scenes.heading: 22 INT. HAYLOFT - AFTERNOON 22 22
scenes.summary: The women debate leaving versus fleeing, with Salome declaring she'd rather shoot each man in the heart than flee, while Mariche criticizes Salome for correcting Greta's word choice.

scenes.heading: 23 EXT. NEITJE’S BEDROOM - NIGHT - TWO WEEKS AGO 23 23
scenes.summary: Neitje and Autje see a young man with a spray can climbing a ladder to their bedroom window, they scream, and he scrambles down as voices discuss that only one was caught but he named others.

scenes.heading: 24 INT. HAYLOFT - AFTERNOON 24 24
scenes.summary: The women debate whether the captured man might be lying about others, with Salome passionately arguing they know they were attacked and must protect their children regardless of who is guilty.

scenes.heading: 25 EXT. ROAD - MAGIC HOUR - TWO WEEKS EARLIER 25 25
scenes.summary: Salome walks exhausted down a long dusty road carrying her sleeping daughter on her shoulders as they become small specks in the distance.

scenes.heading: 26 INT. HAYLOFT - EARLY AFTERNOON 26 26
scenes.summary: Salome mentions hiding antibiotics in Miep's applesauce while Agata and Greta recite a prayer about the Lord being gracious and compassionate.

scenes.heading: 27 INT. GRETA’S BEDROOM - DAWN - ONE YEAR EARLIER 27 27
scenes.summary: Greta opens her mouth and blood comes out as she looks down at her bloody teeth in her hand.

scenes.heading: 28 INT. HAYLOFT - EARLY AFTERNOON 28 28
scenes.summary: Neitje and Autje look mortified by the singing while Greta puts her teeth back in and encourages Autje to sing, then Agata calls for a break and the women climb down the ladder.

scenes.heading: 29 EXT. PADDOCK - MID-AFTERNOON 29 29
scenes.summary: Neitje braids Autje's hair in the paddock.

scenes.heading: 30 EXT. BARN - MID-AFTERNOON 30 30
scenes.summary: Mejal smokes a cigarette while watching Melvin/Nettie supervise children, then discusses with Autje whether Nettie will always be like a man now.

scenes.heading: 31 INT. NETTIE/MELVIN'S BEDROOM - SUNRISE 31 31
scenes.summary: Melvin/Nettie, in a nightie and covered in blood from the waist down, stares at something offscreen on the floor.

scenes.heading: 32 INT. NETTIE/MELVIN'S BEDROOM - MORNING - FIVE DAYS EARLIER 32 32
scenes.summary: Melvin/Nettie smears blood over the bedroom walls while hysterical.

scenes.heading: 33 EXT. SHED - MORNING - FIVE DAYS EARLIER 33 33
scenes.summary: Melvin/Nettie, still covered in blood, sits by the shed and tells his brother through the slats about losing a baby, saying it was small but intact and that he loved it.
scenes.turningPointNote: Reveals the devastating personal cost of the assaults - Nettie's miscarriage and trauma that leads to her transformation

scenes.heading: 34 EXT. FIELD - AFTERNOON 34 34
scenes.summary: Nettie/Melvin bandages a little girl's knee while Mejal and Autje continue watching and discussing how she speaks to children but not adults anymore.

scenes.heading: 35 EXT. BARN - AFTERNOON 35 35
scenes.summary: Autje pumps water while August awkwardly tells her about WWII volunteers riding stationary bikes in bomb shelters, then she demonstrates swinging a water pail in a complete circle.

scenes.heading: 36 INT. MARICHE’S KITCHEN - AFTERNOON 36 36
scenes.summary: Mariche tends to her eight children while a census truck with a loudspeaker and 'California Dreaming' playing passes by, making her look unsettled.

scenes.heading: EXT. MARICHE’S HOUSE 36A
scenes.summary: The census truck passes by Mariche's house and continues down the road.

scenes.heading: 37 INT. GRETA’S KITCHEN - AFTERNOON 37 37
scenes.summary: Greta makes bread with four grandchildren while the census loudspeaker announces that all residents must come out to be counted.

scenes.heading: 38 INT. SALOME'S HOUSE - AFTERNOON 38 38
scenes.summary: Salome crushes a pill into applesauce and feeds it to Miep while Aaron tickles Miep and Salome smiles.

scenes.heading: 39 INT. SCARFACE JANZ’S SEWING ROOM- AFTERNOON 39 39
scenes.summary: Scarface Janz sews with Anna, Helena, and two other daughters, looking up at the sound of the census voice and remaining still.

scenes.heading: 40 EXT. CHICKEN COOP - AFTERNOON 40 40
scenes.summary: Agata collects eggs and looks up at the booming census voice but pays little attention.

scenes.heading: 41 INT. GRETA’S HORSE BARN - AFTERNOON 41 41
scenes.summary: Autje and Neitje pour water and feed hay to Ruth and Cheryl, then hear the census truck approach and leave their work to gravitate toward the music.

scenes.heading: 42 EXT. WASHHOUSE LATE AFTERNOON 42 42
scenes.summary: Mejal braids her daughter's hair under hanging laundry while other children play and wash clothes, hearing but ignoring the loudspeaker.

scenes.heading: 43 INT HAYLOFT - LATE AFTERNOON 43 43
scenes.summary: August sees Autje and Neitje approach the stopped census truck and flirt with the driver while 'California Dreaming' plays, then the truck drives away.

scenes.heading: 44 INT. HAYLOFT - LATE AFTERNOON 44 44
scenes.summary: August sits alone in the empty hayloft singing 'California Dreaming' until the women return with food baskets, then Autje climbs up making hysterical moans.

scenes.heading: 45 INT. SCHOOLHOUSE - MORNING 45 45
scenes.summary: August looks at his male students' faces while the women's voices discuss in voiceover whether it's men or a way of seeing the world that prevents their freedom and safety.

scenes.heading: 46 INT. HAYLOFT - LATE AFTERNOON 46 46
scenes.summary: Neitje and Autje look full of grief as the women contemplate their relationships with the boys and men they love, while Miep sleeps in Salome's arms.

scenes.heading: 47 EXT FIELD - MAGIC HOUR 47 47
scenes.summary: The sun is lower in the sky as cows moo and dogs bark in the distance.

scenes.heading: 48 INT. HAYLOFT - MAGIC HOUR 48 48
scenes.summary: Melvin/Nettie gently takes sleeping Miep from Salome's arms and carries her down the ladder while the women watch in silence, then Ona goes for water followed awkwardly by August.

scenes.heading: 49 EXT. FIELD - MAGIC HOUR 49 49
scenes.summary: Melvin/Nettie watches children play while cradling sleeping Miep in his arms, looking down at her tenderly.

scenes.heading: 50 EXT. PUMP - MAGIC HOUR 50 50
scenes.summary: Ona vomits at the pump while August helps her drink water from his cupped hands, then they stand close together in silence as she wipes tears from his face.

scenes.heading: 51 INT. HAYLOFT - MAGIC HOUR 51 51
scenes.summary: The women discuss whether men and older boys should be allowed to leave with them, with Mariche rejecting August's academic language and debate continuing about young boys and simple-minded boys.

scenes.heading: 52 INT. MEJAL’S ROOM - MORNING - ONE YEAR EARLIER 52 52
scenes.summary: Mejal stands up from bed one year earlier, blood dripping to the floor, then begins screaming as Agata prays over her voice-over.

scenes.heading: 53 INT. HAYLOFT - TWILIGHT 53 53
scenes.summary: Salome holds Mejal as she recovers from her attack using cigarette smoke, while Mariche criticizes Mejal for drawing attention and Mejal defends herself.

scenes.heading: 54 INT. ONA’S BEDROOM - SUNRISE - 7 MONTHS EARLIER 54 54
scenes.summary: Ona wakes up seven months earlier barely able to move with blood stains on her bed, calling for her mother while expressing love for her unborn child.
scenes.turningPointNote: Key revelation of Ona's assault and pregnancy from rape

scenes.heading: 55 INT. HAYLOFT - TWILIGHT 55 55
scenes.summary: Ona speaks about loving her innocent child and the innocence of the child's father when born, leading to discussion about forgiveness requiring distance from the men.

scenes.heading: 56 EXT ROAD - MORNING 56 56
scenes.summary: Greta drives her buggy down the dangerous northern road, her horses moving erratically then finding focus as she speaks in voice-over.

scenes.heading: 57 INT. GRETA’S KITCHEN - MORNING 57 57
scenes.summary: Greta feeds porridge to her one-year-old granddaughter on her lap, making her laugh while speaking about loving properly and keeping peace.

scenes.heading: 58 INT. GRETA’S KITCHEN - MORNING 58 58
scenes.summary: Greta plays hide and seek with her granddaughter, acting childlike and magical while explaining in voice-over that leaving would be an act of faith.

scenes.heading: 59 INT. HAYLOFT - TWILIGHT 59 59
scenes.summary: Greta thinks she's dying until Mejal realizes her glasses just need cleaning, causing laughter, while Mariche argues they should stay and fight rather than leave.

scenes.heading: 60 EXT. WASHHOUSE FENCE - MORNING 60 60
scenes.summary: Salome plays with Aaron by the washhouse fence, grabbing his waist and startling him playfully while Greta speaks in voice-over about the sadness of leaving him behind.

scenes.heading: 61 INT. HAYLOFT - MAGIC HOUR 61 61
scenes.summary: The women comfort Salome about leaving Aaron behind and question why boys aged thirteen and fourteen must be left, asking August for his opinion as their teacher.

scenes.heading: 62 EXT. SCHOOLHOUSE - MORNING 62 62
scenes.summary: Boys at the schoolhouse race and shove each other, first playfully then aggressively, while August describes their reckless urges and physical exuberance in voice-over.

scenes.heading: 63 INT. SALOME’S KITCHEN - MORNING 63 63
scenes.summary: Aaron stares silently into the camera while August's voice-over discusses educating boys through love, intellectual accuracy, and imaginative power using Coleridge's principles.

scenes.heading: 64 INT. HAYLOFT - MAGIC HOUR 64 64
scenes.summary: August recommends boys should leave with the women, Mariche criticizes his verbose response, and Agata proposes all boys under fifteen must accompany the women.

scenes.heading: 65 EXT. PORCH - TWILIGHT 65 65
scenes.summary: Neitje and Autje wake sleeping women on a porch to inform them of the plan while Mejal worries the 'do nothing' women will inform the men.

scenes.heading: 66 INT. SEWING ROOM - TWILIGHT 66 66
scenes.summary: Neitje and Autje approach women in a sewing room including Scarface Janz, who shakes her head almost imperceptibly in endless silence.

scenes.heading: 67 INT. HAYLOFT - TWILIGHT 67 67
scenes.summary: Ona states she must have faith in all the women while Greta warns Mariche to be careful and they prepare to leave the hayloft.

scenes.heading: 68 EXT. FIELD - TWILIGHT 68 68
scenes.summary: Mariche collects her children from the field and sees Klaas beckoning from their house, telling Greta she must go home to avoid drawing attention.

scenes.heading: 69 EXT. COLONY PATH - TWILIGHT 69 69
scenes.summary: Neitje and Autje walk along paths telling women to congregate at sunrise by the wash house and bring everything, including their buggies.

scenes.heading: EXT. COLONY PATH - MOMENTS LATER 69A
scenes.summary: Autje and Neitje continue informing women about meeting at sunrise and needing their buggies, receiving nods of solidarity.

scenes.heading: 70 EXT. COLONY HOUSE - TWILIGHT 70 70
scenes.summary: Neitje and Autje tell Clara they leave at sunrise and need both her buggies, causing Clara to catch her breath anxiously about having so much to do.

scenes.heading: 71 INT. SALOME'S KITCHEN - TWILIGHT 71 71
scenes.summary: Salome tells Aaron about the leaving plan while he looks stunned, and she worries that Miep's pills aren't working because they're meant for calves.

scenes.heading: 72 OMITTED 72 72
scenes.summary: Scene is omitted.

scenes.heading: 73 OMITTED 73 73
scenes.summary: Scene is omitted.

scenes.heading: 74 EXT. MARICHE’S HOUSE - NIGHT 74 74
scenes.summary: Autje and Neitje lead horses away from Mariche's house, freezing when a light turns on then continuing when it turns off.

scenes.heading: 75 OMITTED 75 75
scenes.summary: Scene is omitted.

scenes.heading: 76 INT GRETA’S HOUSE- NIGHT 76 76
scenes.summary: Agata, Greta and Mejal quickly load barrels with food supplies including cheese, sausage, bread, flour, eggs and water.

scenes.heading: INT. SALOME'S HOUSE - MIEP’S BEDROOM - NIGHT 76B
scenes.summary: Salome tucks Miep into bed.

scenes.heading: 77 EXT. WASHHOUSE - NIGHT 77 77
scenes.summary: Ona sits on the washhouse roof and calls August to join her, where he shows her a map with a legend of pictures showing rivers, roads, towns and borders.

scenes.heading: 78 EXT. BARN - PRE-DAWN 78 78
scenes.summary: August watches from a window as Ona teaches the other women to find the Southern Cross with their hands as dawn approaches.

scenes.heading: 79 INT. HAYLOFT - EARLY MORNING 79 79
scenes.summary: The women gather in the hayloft waiting for Mariche and Autje, with Greta pacing nervously until Mariche climbs up with a bruised and cut face, helped by Autje.
scenes.turningPointNote: Mariche returns beaten, showing the immediate danger and confirming the urgency of their escape

scenes.heading: 80 EXT. COLONY ROAD - EARLY MORNING 80 80
scenes.summary: A group of women pulling children down a road stop and look toward the distant singing coming from the barn.

scenes.heading: 81 EXT. SCARFACE JANZ’ HOUSE - EARLY MORNING 81 81
scenes.summary: Scarface Janz restrains Anna from running toward the hymn music while Helena's face points toward the sound.

scenes.heading: 82 EXT. FIELD - EARLY MORNING 82 82
scenes.summary: Nettie/Melvin and children in the field listen to the distant singing, with some children joining in while others continue playing obliviously.

scenes.heading: 83 INT. HAYLOFT - EARLY MORNING 83 83
scenes.summary: After singing, August tries to speak but cries instead, then an old man with dementia, Earnest Penner, climbs into the loft asking what's happening.

scenes.heading: 84 EXT. LOWER BARN DOOR - SUNRISE 84 84
scenes.summary: Mejal holds mint under Earnest's nose for him to smell, and he smiles and picks some himself.

scenes.heading: 85 INT. HAYLOFT - SUNRISE 85 85
scenes.summary: The women prepare to leave with Agata saying goodbye tearfully, Salome wanting guns but Agata refusing, and Nettie/Melvin confirming the children are ready.
scenes.turningPointNote: The women make their final decision to leave and begin the actual departure

scenes.heading: 86 INT. HAYLOFT - MORNING 86 86
scenes.summary: August sits alone making a list while surrounded by the women's notes and illustrations on the walls, reflecting on how he'll live without them.

scenes.heading: 87 EXT. FIELD - SUNRISE 87 87
scenes.summary: The sun rises with beams shining through trees and light dancing on a pond as Agata says 'Sun' in voice-over.

scenes.heading: 88 EXT. FIELD - NIGHT 88 88
scenes.summary: The camera stares up at the night sky and milky way as Neitje says 'Stars' in voice-over.

scenes.heading: 89 INT. BARN - DAY 89 89
scenes.summary: A pail sits alone among discarded tools in the barn as Miep says 'Pails' in voice-over.

scenes.heading: 90 EXT. HOUSE - DAY 90 90
scenes.summary: A newborn baby stares at the sky adjusting to light as Autje says 'Birth' in voice-over.

scenes.heading: 91 EXT. FIELDS - MAGIC HOUR 91 91
scenes.summary: A two-year-old sleeps in a cart of cucumbers as Mariche says 'The Harvest' in voice-over.

scenes.heading: 92 INT. SCHOOLHOUSE - DAY 92 92
scenes.summary: A boy's hand writes down a math problem as Mejal says 'Numbers' in voice-over.

scenes.heading: 93 INT. BARN - MORNING 93 93
scenes.summary: August continues writing his list as Ona and Greta name objects in voice-over.

scenes.heading: 94 INT. SALOME’S KITCHEN - MORNING 94 94
scenes.summary: Aaron's face shows tears streaming down as he sobs and shakes his head violently, then a spray can appears and he screams and drops to the floor.

scenes.heading: 95 EXT. SCARFACE JANZ'S HOUSE - MORNING 95 95
scenes.summary: Salome pushes Scarface Janz to the ground and sprays her face with a can, causing her to go immediately unconscious.
scenes.turningPointNote: Salome takes violent action to prevent Scarface Janz from informing on them, crossing a moral line

scenes.heading: 96 INT. KLAAS’ HORSE BARN - MORNING 96 96
scenes.summary: Salome finds Klaas passed out in a horse stall, and when he begins to rouse, she sprays him and closes the stall door.

scenes.heading: 97 EXT. ROAD - MORNING 97 97
scenes.summary: A group of women walk down a road trying to hold onto their hats as the wind picks up, while various characters name objects in voice-over.
scenes.turningPointNote: The women have successfully left the colony and are walking toward their uncertain future

scenes.heading: 98 INT. HAYLOFT - MORNING 98 98
scenes.summary: Salome takes weapons from the tack room and gives August a spray can for protection, revealing she has forced Aaron to come with her and broken the new rules.
scenes.turningPointNote: Salome reveals she has used force against Aaron to make him leave, breaking the community's new rules and crossing a moral line

scenes.heading: 99 EXT. WASH HOUSE ROAD - MORNING 99 99
scenes.summary: The women and children frantically load supplies and board a convoy of buggies, with Anna running scared alongside as they prepare to depart.
scenes.turningPointNote: The women execute their decision to leave, abandoning their community in a desperate exodus

scenes.heading: 100 INT. HAYLOFT - MORNING 100 100
scenes.summary: August watches the convoy leave from the hayloft and lifts his hand in farewell to a hand reaching from the fourth buggy.

scenes.heading: 101 EXT WASH HOUSE ROAD - MORNING 101 101
scenes.summary: The convoy of buggies carrying the women and children travels down the long road and disappears into the distance.

scenes.heading: INT. SALOME'S KITCHEN 101A
scenes.summary: Salome's kitchen sits empty and still.

scenes.heading: INT. GRETA’S KITCHEN 101B
scenes.summary: Greta's kitchen sits empty and still.

scenes.heading: INT. MARICHE’S KITCHEN 101C
scenes.summary: Mariche's kitchen sits empty and still.

scenes.heading: 102 INT SCHOOLHOUSE - MORNING 102 102
scenes.summary: August stands in his schoolroom facing teenage boys in chairs, with some empty seats where younger children used to sit, and opens his mouth to speak.
scenes.turningPointNote: August faces the aftermath of the women's departure, left to continue in the transformed community with only the boys remaining

structuralWarnings.text: 2 of 3 key moments were auto-corrected by Phase 1C
